verdict.headline: Do not transact — wallet is sanctioned on OFAC SDN and linked to the DPRK Lazarus Group.
verdict.reasoning: The sanctions check returned a confirmed match against three programs: OFAC SDN, Lazarus Group, and DPRK, which is a hard veto under our policy and overrides all other signals. While the wallet shows a substantial ETH balance (~101.8 ETH) indicating active use, this is irrelevant in the face of a confirmed sanctions hit — in fact, it may reflect illicit holdings. The labels and web_sentiment endpoints returned only API metadata rather than usable label/reputation data, but no compensating positive signal could override sanctions. Sending funds to this address would expose the user to severe regulatory and legal consequences.

findings.finding: Confirmed sanctions match on OFAC SDN, Lazarus Group, and DPRK programs. Hard veto — do not transact.
Label provider returned only API metadata; no usable entity labels retrieved.
Wallet holds ~101.80 ETH; active balance but does not mitigate sanctions exposure and may represent illicit funds.
Reputation service returned only API metadata; no scam or news hits retrieved.
Not applicable — address is an EOA.

services.rationale: Resource 0 is the only sanctions candidate with a confirmed 0% failure rate AND inputInfoCompleteness of 2/3, plus the lowest price ($0.001), making it clearly superior to all untested or high-failure alternatives.
Resource 1 is the only labels candidate with a confirmed 0% recent failure rate, which is the strongest available signal; all other candidates are untested.

Resource 1 is the only onchain_history candidate with a confirmed 0% failure rate combined with inputInfoCompleteness of 2/3, making it the most reliable choice despite identical pricing to some alternatives.

Resource 2 has a confirmed 0% failure rate (ruling out resource 1 at 100%); among the two 0%-failure options, resource 2's description ('risk score 0-100 for any wallet address') is the closest match to web/reputation sentiment scoring, making it the best fit for the category intent.
